issue_title: FIPS: Failures related to JKS keystore
issue_body:
When running testsuite with Keycloak on Quarkus distribution with FIPS support and BCFIPS security providers (after #14886 merged), there are failures like those below. This is due the JKS not supported keystore type in the FIPS environment with just BCFIPS providers used. The test should be either ignored or adjusted for the work with FIPS based distribution.

The solution might be to introduce something like `KeyStoreTypeSPI` or endpoint for retrieve list of supported keystore types from the server, which will be able to check if various keystore types (JKS, PKCS12, BCFKS) are supported and possibly disable some keystore types in case that security providers don't support them.

```
[ERROR]   JavaKeystoreKeyProviderTest.invalidKeyAlias:136->assertErrror:154
[ERROR]   JavaKeystoreKeyProviderTest.invalidKeyPassword:145->assertErrror:154
[ERROR]   ClientAuthSignedJWTTest.testUploadKeystoreJKS:667->testUploadKeystore:729 expected:<200> but was:<500>

[ERROR]   CredentialsTest.testDownloadKeystore:201 » InternalServerError HTTP 500 Internal Server Error
[ERROR]   CredentialsTest.testGenerateAndDownloadKeystore:228 » InternalServerError HTTP 500 Internal Server Error
[ERROR]   CredentialsTest.testUploadKeyAndCertificate:127 » InternalServerError HTTP 500 Internal Server Error

[ERROR]   JavaKeystoreKeyProviderTest.create:93 » WebApplication Create method returned status Bad Request (Code: 400); expected status: Created (201). Response body: {"errorMessage":"Failed to load keys. KeyStore error on server. JKS not found"}
[ERROR]   ClientAuthSignedJWTTest.testClientWithGeneratedKeysJKS:593->testClientWithGeneratedKeys:623 » InternalServerError HTTP 500 Internal Server Error
```

issue_title: FIPS: Failures related to Kerberos/SPNEGO (introduce "KERBEROS" feature)

When running testsuite with Keycloak on Quarkus distribution with FIPS support and BCFIPS security providers (after #14886), there are failures like those below. This is probably due the fact that Kerberos/SPNEGO doesn't work with just BCFIPS security providers. issue_title: [CVE-2021-3914] Cross-site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in io.smallrye:smallrye-health-ui
issue_body:
# Status: Not affected

Keycloak was [upgraded to Quarkus 2.13.2.Final](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/14834), the issue was originally fixed on Quarkus 2.7.5. See: https://access.redhat.com/errata/RHSA-2022:4623

# Overview

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Cross-site Scripting (XSS) due to an improper sanitization of user inputs.

# Details

A cross-site scripting attack occurs when the attacker tricks a legitimate web-based application or site to accept a request as originating from a trusted source.

This is done by escaping the context of the web application; the web application then delivers that data to its users along with other trusted dynamic content, without validating it. The browser unknowingly executes malicious script on the client side (through client-side languages; usually JavaScript or HTML) in order to perform actions that are otherwise typically blocked by the browser’s Same Origin Policy.

Injecting malicious code is the most prevalent manner by which XSS is exploited; for this reason, escaping characters in order to prevent this manipulation is the top method for securing code against this vulnerability.

Escaping means that the application is coded to mark key characters, and particularly key characters included in user input, to prevent those characters from being interpreted in a dangerous context. For example, in HTML, `<` can be coded as `&lt`; and `>` can be coded as `&gt`; in order to be interpreted and displayed as themselves in text, while within the code itself, they are used for HTML tags. If malicious content is injected into an application that escapes special characters and that malicious content uses `<` and `>` as HTML tags, those characters are nonetheless not interpreted as HTML tags by the browser if they’ve been correctly escaped in the application code and in this way the attempted attack is diverted.

The most prominent use of XSS is to steal cookies (source: OWASP HttpOnly) and hijack user sessions, but XSS exploits have been used to expose sensitive information, enable access to privileged services and functionality and deliver malware.

issue_title: With JDK17, AuthzClientTest and Quarkus runtime configuration tests fail with a reflection access error
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

With JDK17 on Quarkus, AuthzClientTest and Quarkus runtime configuration tests fail with a reflection access error

```
InaccessibleObject Unable to make field private final java.util.Map java.util.Collections$UnmodifiableMap.m accessible: module java.base does not "opens java.util" to unnamed module
```

### Version

keycloak 20.0-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

We would want the tests which were passing with the previously supported JDK (JDK11) to also succeed on JDK17 without issue

### Actual behavior

Below tests fail due to a reflection access to map object, from JDK16 on wards, there are deeper restrictions on the ability to use reflection and encounter an Illegal Reflective Access warning, or even a runtime error such as java.lang.reflect.InaccessibleObjectException when trying to manipulate the Map behind the system's environment variables.

```
org.keycloak.testsuite.authz.AuthzClientTest
org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.configuration.test.ConfigurationTest
```

### How to Reproduce?

Run the below commands with setting your local hosts JDK and JAVA_HOME to jdk17 and observe for test results.

AuthzClientTest
```
mvn clean install  -Dtest=org.keycloak.testsuite.authz.AuthzClientTest -Pauth-server-quarkus
```
ConfigurationTest
```
mvn clean install  -Dtest=org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.configuration.test.ConfigurationTest -Pauth-server-quarkus
```

### Anything else?

I was able to make the tests work with the below change to the existing jdk9 maven profile inside the root [pom.xml](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/pom.xml#L1906)

@mposolda If you think this change makes sense to be part of the main branch, I can create a PR for the same, lmk

@nehachopra27 thanks for the help with the investigation and suggestion for the fix

```
        <profile>
            <id>jdk9</id>
            <activation>
                <jdk>[9,)</jdk>
            </activation>
            <properties>
                <surefire.system.args>
                    --add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED
                    --add-opens=java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED
                </surefire.system.args>
            </properties>
        </profile>
```

issue_title: [CVE-2022-25857] Denial of Service (DoS) due to missing nested depth limitation for collections in org.yaml:snakeyaml
issue_body:
# Status: Not affected

# Overview
[org.yaml:snakeyaml](https://code.google.com/p/snakeyaml/source/browse/) is a YAML 1.1 parser and emitter for Java.

Affected versions ( < 1.31) of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) due to missing nested depth limitation for collections.

Keycloak is currently using Liquibase version 4.8.0, which is affected by the vunerability.

# Details
Denial of Service (DoS) describes a family of attacks, all aimed at making a system inaccessible to its intended and legitimate users.

Unlike other vulnerabilities, DoS attacks usually do not aim at breaching security. Rather, they are focused on making websites and services unavailable to genuine users resulting in downtime.

One popular Denial of Service vulnerability is DDoS (a Distributed Denial of Service), an attack that attempts to clog network pipes to the system by generating a large volume of traffic from many machines.

When it comes to open source libraries, DoS vulnerabilities allow attackers to trigger such a crash or crippling of the service by using a flaw either in the application code or from the use of open source libraries.

Two common types of DoS vulnerabilities:

High CPU/Memory Consumption- An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to take a disproportionate amount of time to process. For example, [commons-fileupload:commons-fileupload](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/security/code-scanning/SNYK-JAVA-COMMONSFILEUPLOAD-30082).

Crash - An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to crash. For Example, [npm ws package](https://snyk.io/vuln/npm:ws:20171108)

# Remediation
Upgrade org.yaml:snakeyaml to version 1.31 or higher.

In Keycloak the dependency is coming from org.liquibase:liquibase-core. This dependency has to be upgraded to version 4.16.0 or higher.

issue_title: IDENTITY_PROVIDER_FIRST_LOGIN is never triggered
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The event `IDENTITY_PROVIDER_FIRST_LOGIN` can be useful, if one would like to trigger some action whenever there is one (by creating a custom EventListenerProvider), or simply for auditing purposes by storing it. 

It seems like the event is used  here: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/LoginActionsService.java#L759

However, it seems like the event is never triggered because the `.success()` method is never called. 

### Version

19.0.2

### Expected behavior

The `IDENTITY_PROVIDER_FIRST_LOGIN` should be triggered

### Actual behavior

The `IDENTITY_PROVIDER_FIRST_LOGIN` is not triggered

### How to Reproduce?

* create a demo realm
* add an identity provider of your choice (I tested using Google)
* activate event auditing, by going to Realm settings -> Events -> User events settings -> and then activate save events and hit save. 
* navigate to the account console from a different browser session (e.g private window): http://localhost:8081/auth/realms/YOUR_TEST_REALM_NAME/account/#/
* try logging in using the identity provider you configured
* Go back to the admin console -> and check the events that got triggered. 

You will find the following events:
* CODE_TO_TOKEN
* LOGIN
* REGISTER

but not the IDENTITY_PROVIDER_FIRST_LOGIN

### Anything else?

This can be fixed simply by adding `event.success()` (checkout the PR for more details)
The only thing to note is that the user is not created yet at this point, so the user field in the event will be null / empty. Here is an example entry:
![localhost_8081_auth_admin_master_console_](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6265563/197346510-5f17d752-913b-4720-b7af-2c424d1a9ea2.png)

trigger IDENTITY_PROVIDER_FIRST_LOGIN (and UPDATE_PROFILE ) event when identity provider flow succeeds (#15100)

closes #15098

issue_title: Close the session within resteasy boundaries
issue_body:
### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

As a follow-up on https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/15152.

In order to avoid potential deadlocks in the event loop thread (even though we always dispatch requests to a worker thread) we should close the session within rest easy boundaries.

This basically means closing the session before the response is written to the underlying HTTP connection.

### Version

19.0.3

### Expected behavior

Close session before the response is written to the underlying HTTP connection.

### Actual behavior

The session is closed when the response is still being written to the underlying HTTP connection.

### How to Reproduce?

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/15152

### Anything else?

_No response_

Close the session within resteasy boundaries (#15193)

issue_title: JPA Map store JDBC transactions not rolled back on Hibernate transaction rollback when running in Quarkus
issue_body:
### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

Given a setup with Quarkus and JPA map storage, a transaction is not rolled back. Instead, the changes are written to the database.


### Version

19.0.2

### Expected behavior

When a transaction is marked for rollback, it should be rolled back.


### Actual behavior

When the rollback is triggered to the Hibernate transaction, that is not propagated to the underlying PostgreSQL DB connection. 
Instead, when the DB connection is returned, it is being committed.


### How to Reproduce?

* Start Quarkus as described in discussion #9596 with JPA map storage
* Login and select a client
* Change the “Valid redirect URIs” to contain a “#text” (an anchor) at the end
* Press “Save” -> an error message appears
* Press reload in the browser to load the changes from the database -> the changes have been persisted to the database


### Anything else?

Part of the cause might be that Hibernate is still using JTA for transactions, but the JDBC connection is not bound to the JTA transaction; instead it needs to be handled manually.

Possible remedies: 
* Manually commit/rollback the JDBC transaction in JpaTransactionWrapper with something like: 
em.unwrap(SessionImpl.class).doWork(Connection::rollback);
* Use JTA all the way for JPA Map storage in Quarkus 
* Use RESOURCE_LOCAL transaction type
commit_message:
Use RESOURCE_LOCAL transactions for JPA map storage

Closes #15248

issue_title: Disabling hostname strict in prod doesn't disable https
issue_body:
### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Disabling `hostname-strict` in prod mode still results in URLs with https when running locally, which is causing the admin console to not load.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

With `hostname-strict=false` it is expected that URLs are fully dynamic, but they still switch to https.

### Actual behavior

It seems the hostname is not fully dynamic with only `hostname-strict=false`, and they still use URLs.

### How to Reproduce?

Run Keycloak in prod mode locally using:

```
bin/kc.sh start --http-enabled=true --hostname-strict=false
```

Then check http://localhost:8080/realms/master/.well-known/openid-configuration, and observe:

```
"issuer":"https://localhost:8443/realms/master"
```

As hostname strict is disabled it should be using dynamic resolution of the URL, but this is not fully the case, only parts of the URL is dynamic.

Now run with:

```
bin/kc.sh start --http-enabled=true --hostname-strict=false --hostname-strict-https=false
```

This time around http://localhost:8080/realms/master/.well-known/openid-configuration is using http URLs:

```
"issuer":"http://localhost:8080/realms/master"
```

However, it shouldn't be necessary to include `hostname-strict-https=false` in this case, in fact this is actually slightly worse since it's an internal option that is not documented.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Disabling strict https if hostname is diasabled

Closes #15287

issue_title: Client-scoped roles are not assignable when using admin fine-grained permissions
issue_body:
### Area

admin/fine-grained-permissions

### Describe the bug

We're experimenting with the preview feature admin fine-grained permissions. We've been following the official [documentation ](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_fine_grain_permissions). Overall, everything seems to work well.

We've been able to set up a user that's only allowed to manage a client, assign realm roles, and group memberships (among others).

We are however having issues getting the client-scoped role mapping assignment permission to work. We've tried both the fine-grained role permissions as described in the [documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_fine_grain_permissions), and the client-wide `map-roles` permission.

The user we're testing with has the `view-users` role and is able to navigate to the `Role mapping` tab for any user. We're also able to click the `Assign role` button. However, we're unable to list client-scoped roles associated with clients for which we applied a user policy to the `map-roles` permission.

We do see a call going out to the following URL but no results are returned:

```
https://localhost:9000/auth/admin/realms/test/admin-ui-available-roles/users/f12b1055-bcc6-4c85-b38b-825876452dfa?first=0&max=11&search=
```

![no-roles-to-assign](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5998869/199542097-aa1f35e8-59e3-44a1-aa67-036962fa76aa.gif)

One interesting observation is that when a regular admin user would assign a client-scoped role to our dummy user, the user that was granted the `map-roles` permission is able to unassign that role.

![unassign-role](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5998869/199543319-0aa7c971-9db4-4bef-96b5-d73079b55faa.gif)


### Version

19.0.3 and 20.0.0

### Expected behavior

A user that has the `map-roles` permission (either on an individual client-scoped role or on an entire client) should be able to assign client-scoped roles.

### Actual behavior

No client-scoped roles are listed in the `Assign role` dialog

### How to Reproduce?

Follow the official [documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#restrict-user-role-mapping).

### Anything else?

_No response_

fixed fine grain permissions (#16668)

fixes: #3700

issue_title: No email send on TOTP/Authenticator app removal
issue_body:
### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

When a TOTP device/authenticator app is removed via account console, no email is send via the email event listener.
This worked with the old account console, but does not with the new account console.

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

An email should be send to the user that notifies the user about the removal of TOTP device/Authenticator app.

### Actual behavior

No email is send when the new account console is used.
With the old account console the email is send.

### How to Reproduce?

Create a file named `docker-compose.yml` with this content:
```yaml
version: '3'
services:
  keycloak:
    container_name: keycloak
    image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:20.0.1
    environment:
      KC_SPI_EVENTS_LISTENER_EMAIL_ENABLED: "true"
      KC_SPI_EVENTS_LISTENER_EMAIL_INCLUDE_EVENTS: UPDATE_TOTP,REMOVE_TOTP
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN: admin
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD: admin
      DEBUG_PORT: '*:8787'
      DEBUG: 'true'
    command: ['start-dev', '--debug']
    ports:
      - 8080:8080
      - 8787:8787
  mail:
    container_name: mail
    image: maildev/maildev
    ports:
      - 1080:1080

```

- Start the docker containers via `docker-compose up`.
- Navigate to http://localhost:8080/admin/master/console/#/
- Login with user `admin` and password `admin`
- Navigate to `Users -> admin`
  - Add `admin@local.local` as email address
  - Mark email as verified
  - Save the changes
- Navigate to `Realm settings -> Email`
  - Add `keycloak@local.local` as from address
  - Add `mail` as SMTP host
  - Add `1025` as SMTP port
  - Save the changes
  - Test connection (should succeed)
- Navigate to `Realm settings -> Events -> Event Listeners`
  - Add `email` to the list of event listeners
  - Save changes
- Navigate to http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/#/security/signingin
  - Click `Setup authenticator application`
  - Register authenticator app via barcode
- Navigate to http://localhost:1080/#/
  - You should see an email with subject `Update OTP`
- Navigate http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/#/security/signingin
  - Remove the authenticator application
- Navigate to http://localhost:1080/#/
  - refresh the page
  - ⚠️ No email with subject `Remove OTP` is shown
- In the admin console navigate to `Realm settings -> themes` (http://localhost:8080/admin/master/console/#/master/realm-settings/themes)
  - Change the `Account theme` to `keycloak` (old account management console)
  - Save changes
- Navigate to http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/totp
  - Register authenticator app via barcode
 - Navigate to http://localhost:1080/#/
  - refresh the page
  - An additional email with subject `Update OTP` is shown
- Navigate to http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/totp
  - Delete the configured authenticator
- Navigate to http://localhost:1080/#/
  - refresh the page
  - ✔️ Email with subject `Remove OTP` is shown

### Anything else?

The old account console uses this endpoint to delte the authenticator app: `http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/totp`
The new account console uses this endpoint instead: `http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/credentials/<credential_id>`

It looks like the credential endpoint does not fire the `REMOVE_TOTP` event.

issue_title: User Federation: getReadable() can throw a NPR for a federated user if the user has no attributes
issue_body:
### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

for some imported users  loaded from a federated storage source (and that have no attributes), the Attributes class throws an NPE here https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/Attributes.java#L141

This happens if the LegacyAttributes are the provided implementation. This is default implementation unless declarative user profile is used ( declarative user profile is considered as an experiemental feature)

here to be more precise: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/LegacyAttributes.java#L49

This leads to an NPE and also the user details page in the admin console cannot be loaded 



### Version

20.0.0

### Expected behavior

No error is thrown even if the federated users have empty attributes map 

I think it's better to return an empty map rather than null to avoid NPE and also not having to depend on a feature activated

### Actual behavior

NPE is thrown, and the following message is shown 


![localhost_8080_auth_admin_master_console_ (2)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6265563/201541912-2ba65acf-eaa5-41da-8895-50b216fae13a.png)




### How to Reproduce?

This requires creating a custom user provider that implements `UserLookupProvider`

if the UserModel returned by either getUserById, getUserByUsername, getUserByEmail has empty attributes map which is possible because not all users have attributes, you will see an error in the admin console when navigating to the user details in the users admin section 



### Anything else?

This can be avoided by activating the declartive user profile (-Dkeycloak.profile.feature.declarative_user_profile=enabled) which uses the 

DefaultAttributes (https://github.dev/keycloak/keycloak/blob/009ca27a387146ac24e0f82a8e2919af5fe026b4/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/DefaultAttributes.java#L1) as an implementation. 

However the declarative user profile is only experimental for now.

### Version

many users can get an access token at the same time (X.509 authentication)

### How to Reproduce?

 ```
/* load cert */
  private def getKeyStore(jksFile : File): KeyManagerFactory = {

    val clientFis: FileInputStream = new FileInputStream(jksFile)
```

issue_title: Unknown bind DN using LDAP anonymous bind aka bind type none
issue_body:
### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

I'm trying to connect Keycloak 19.0.3 to OpenLDAP.

With my LDAP settings, when I test the connection, it works.  "Successfully connected to LDAP"

When I use the test authentication button, I get an error.  "Error when trying to connect to LDAP. See server.log for details. LDAP test error"

I am trying to use anonymous bind (bind type none in Keycloak)  talking to LDAP, which works for other LDAP clients, e.g. sssd or php-ldap.

The log tells me: 2022-11-15 00:19:36,532 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.managers.LDAPServerCapabilitiesManager] (executor-thread-134) Unknown bind DN

### Version

19.0.3

### Expected behavior

The authentication test should work.

issue_title: Able to modify built-in flow
issue_body:
### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

I am able to add a sub-flow to a built-in flow (e.g. registration). See bottom of screenshot:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/97203210/202324055-5ee7e703-5a57-4f92-8ca7-ce7509601973.png)




### Version

20.0.0

### Expected behavior

Since I am unable to add steps to a "Built-in" flow (resulting in "Could not update flow: It is illegal to add execution to a built in flow"), I assume the built-in flows are meant to be immutable. 

Therefore, I would not expect to be able to add a sub-flow.



### Actual behavior

I am able to add a sub-flow to a built-in flow.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Go to Authentication in admin UI.
2. Select "registration (Built-in)" flow.

4. Leave the defaults with the exception of providing a required name.
5. Click "Add"

_No response_

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

    Synchronization settings

       Sync Registrations Off
   Batch size 
       Periodic full sync Off
       Periodic changed users sync  Off
2 - When i create the first user in my ldap. the search user in keycloak UI return duplicate user (the same use)  ==> KO

20.0.1

returned two result for the first user creation in ldap
